concepts.lesson: A point is a location in space, but it has no size or dimension. It can be represented by a single letter, such as A.
key_points: Points have no size or dimension

concepts.lesson: A line is a set of points that extend infinitely in two directions. It has one dimension, length.
A line segment is a part of a line that has two endpoints and extends between them. It has a fixed length.
key_points: Line segments are parts of lines

concepts.lesson: A ray is a part of a line that extends infinitely in one direction from an endpoint. It has a fixed length.
key_points: Rays extend infinitely in one direction

concepts.lesson: An angle is formed by two lines or line segments that intersect at a point. It has a measure, which can be acute, right, obtuse, or straight.
A straight angle is an angle whose measure is 180 degrees. It can be formed by two lines or line segments that intersect at a point.
key_points: Straight angles have measures of 180 degrees
Straight angles are formed by intersection
